This suggests that each item has a fixed direction: some items can only grow, some can only shrink, and some can do both.
Actually, maybe the items' initial sizes are different, and the grow/shrink actions move them toward a target size. If the item is already at or past the target, the action has no effect.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to make all items reach their target sizes, and the red dot indicates progress. When all items are at their target sizes, the level is complete.
The red dot is at (61, 40), and the target cross is at (31, 52). The horizontal distance is 12 cols. Maybe the red dot's position encodes the total "error" or "distance to target" across all items.
Actually, I just realized something. The red dot at row 61 moves when I change the items. It's like a slider or indicator. Maybe the goal is to move the red dot to align with the target cross.
The red dot is at col 40, and the target is at col 52. I need to move it right by 12 cols. But I can't grow orange further.
Let me try growing green to see if it moves the red dot.
